name: Mocha Fudge Pie
recipeIngredient: 1/2 box (19.85-ounce) light fudge brownie mix (about 2 cups)
3 tablespoons Kahlua or other coffee-flavored liqueur, divided
1 package (3.9-ounce) chocolate-flavored instant pudding-and-pie filling mix
3 cups Frozen reduced-calorie whipped topping, thawed and divided
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine hot water and 2 teaspoons of coffee granules in a medium bowl; stir well.
2. Add brownie mix, teaspoon of vanilla, and egg whites; stir until well blended.
3. Pour mixture into a 9-inch pie plate coated with cooking spray.
4. Bake at 325 degrees for 22 minutes. Let crust cool completely.
5. Combine milk, 2 tablespoons of Kahlua, 1 teaspoon of coffee granules, remaining teaspoon of vanilla, and pudding mix in a bowl; beat at medium speed of a mixer for 1 minute.
6. Gently fold in 1-1/2 cups of whipped topping. Spread pudding mixture evenly over brownie crust.
7. Combine remaining 1 tablespoon of Kahlua and remaining 1 teaspoon of coffee granules in a bowl; stir well.
8. Gently fold in remaining 1-1/2 cups of whipped topping. Spread whipped topping mixture evenly over pudding mixture.
9. Garnish with chocolate curls, if desired. Serve immediately, or store loosely covered in refrigerator.
Nonalcoholic Mocha Version:
When making the pudding mixture, substitute 2 tablespoons of 1% low-fat milk for the Kahlua. In the topping, omit the Kahlua and dissolve the coffee granules in 1 tablespoon of water.
Note: Store remaining brownie mix in a zip-top heavy-duty plastic bag in refrigerator; reserved brownie mix can be used for another pie or to make a small pan of brownies. To make brownies, combine reserved brownie mix (about 2 cups), 1/4 cup of water, and 1 lightly beaten egg white in a bowl. Stir just until combined. Spread into an 8-inch square pan coated with vegetable cooking spray. Bake at 350 degrees for 23 to 25 minutes.
